behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is not just local parsing; it is whether the model actually outputs the right signature from natural language. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required beyond the model call. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: a stubbed LLM would always return a fixed correct signature and hide real signature mismatch failures. MULTI-STEP: not required by this behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text only requires one generation decision ("generated function signature matches..."), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real LLM with several interface-sensitive prompts and assert the produced function signature exactly matches expected parameter names/types and return type.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior spans generation, test execution, and retry orchestration. REAL-INFRA: test execution can be local, but the core risk is not OS-specific; it is iterative agent behavior. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: fixed stubs hide whether model outputs actually become correct. MULTI-STEP: explicitly includes "first or a subsequent attempt," which requires sequential agentic decisions across attempts. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text requires success across iterative attempts, not just one model output.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real LLM and real test runner on tasks where first attempt may fail; assert the loop can pass on attempt 1..3 and reports passing suite when refinement succeeds.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the bug appears at module handoff (test runner output -> prompt builder/orchestrator), not purely inside one algorithmic function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; in-process wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking at module boundaries can still catch this if prompt payload is asserted. LLM-DEP: correctness here is prompt content inclusion, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no need to validate multiple model decisions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about "includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt," which is deterministic payload propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate a failed test result, run refinement orchestration with a stub LLM transport, and assert the second-call prompt contains the exact failure messages.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is retry-state orchestration plus result aggregation across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is inherently required; deterministic failing stubs are enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not necessarily hide this; forced failures expose off-by-one and missing-attempt-history bugs. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality; any forced failure works. MULTI-STEP: multiple iterations occur, but they are control-flow retries, not required agentic quality checks. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on stopping/structured error after "3 failed attempts," which can be validated with deterministic failures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + attempt store + error serializer, force three consecutive failures, and assert exactly three attempts are recorded and returned with per-attempt test results in structured error format.

behaviors.description: The full loop — generate, test, refine, test again — produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure emerges only across stage handoffs (initial generation -> failure feedback -> refined generation). REAL-INFRA: not primarily OS/network-specific; key risk is sequential agent behavior. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: stubs hide whether refinement actually improves solution quality. MULTI-STEP: behavior explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence and successful end-to-end recovery. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires the "full loop" and improvement after refinement, i.e., more than one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with a real LLM on a benchmark prompt known to commonly fail in one shot, verify first attempt fails tests and subsequent refined attempt passes within retry limit.
